title: Dolphin Watching Private Boat Hire in Marbella: 2026 Costs
html:
Private dolphin watching boat hire in Marbella starts at around EUR 350 for a half-day, with most trips leaving from Puerto Banús marina or Marbella's own harbour. BoatHire24 lists skippered boats that take small groups out along the Costa del Sol to look for resident dolphins.

Where to set off in Marbella

Marbella sits on a sheltered stretch of the Costa del Sol, and most private dolphin trips begin from one of a handful of well-known harbours. Each gives quick access to water where bottlenose and common dolphins are often seen within a few nautical miles of the coast.

  • Puerto Banús: the glamorous marina just west of town, the busiest departure point for skippered charters.
  • Puerto Deportivo de Marbella: the central harbour close to the old town, handy for groups staying in Marbella itself.
  • Puerto Cabopino: a smaller, quieter marina to the east, near the Artola dunes.
  • La Bajadilla: the working port beside the centre, used by several local boats.
  • Estepona: a short drive west, a calmer alternative when Marbella is fully booked.

From any of these, the captain heads to the dolphin grounds, often a run of three to eight nautical miles offshore depending on where the pods were sighted that morning.

Which boat to rent in Marbella for dolphin watching?

For a private dolphin trip, choose a stable, shaded boat with room to move and a skipper who knows the local pods. Families usually want comfort and a swim platform, while smaller groups can go faster and lighter. The options below cover most budgets and party sizes.

  1. RIB or open speedboat: best for couples and small groups chasing pods quickly, seats up to 8, from EUR 350 a half-day.
  2. Day cruiser: a comfortable all-rounder with a cabin and sun pads, seats up to 10, from EUR 600 a day.
  3. Sailing catamaran: steady, shaded and quiet, ideal for relaxed family watching, seats up to 12, from EUR 900 a day.
  4. Flybridge motor yacht: spacious decks and a high helm for spotting, seats up to 12, from EUR 1,500 a day.
  5. Luxury motor yacht: a crewed option for a private celebration on the water; you can charter this luxury yacht, the Maiora 26m, which seats up to 12, from EUR 6,000 a day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella?

Expect anywhere from about EUR 35 per seat on a shared tour to several thousand euros a day for a crewed yacht in 2026. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own. You can book a boat online once you have compared a few listings and dates.

  • Shared dolphin or coastal tour: from around EUR 35 to EUR 60 per person.
  • Small private motorboat, half-day: from about EUR 350 in EUR.
  • Sailing catamaran, full day: from about EUR 900 in EUR.
  • Crewed luxury yacht, full day: from about EUR 1,500 to EUR 6,000 in EUR, depending on size and crew.

When is the best season for dolphin watching in Marbella?

The high season runs from May to October, when seas are calmest and dolphin sightings most reliable. Sea conditions are gentlest in the morning before the breeze builds. The local levante, an easterly wind, can stir up chop and push trips earlier in the day.

For the calmest water and the best light, book the first departure of the day in June or September, when the Costa del Sol is warm but the afternoon levante has not yet set in. Ask your skipper which marina they will leave from, as morning sea state can vary between Puerto Banús and Cabopino.

Spain allows many smaller boats to be hired without a licence when a qualified skipper is aboard, which is how private dolphin trips work; the operator confirms the exact requirements for each boat before you book.

At a glance

  • Price band: from about EUR 35 per person shared, EUR 350 and up for a private half-day.
  • Peak months: May to October.
  • Typical trip length: three to four hours.
  • Guest capacity: up to 12 on most private boats.
  • Currency: EUR.
  • Departure: Puerto Banús, Marbella harbour, Cabopino or Estepona.

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Are dolphins guaranteed on a Marbella trip?

Sightings are very common between May and October, but no responsible operator guarantees them because dolphins are wild. Most Marbella skippers know the pods well and many offer a second trip if no dolphins appear on the day.

How close can the boat get to the dolphins?

Skippers keep a respectful distance and let the dolphins approach, which they often do out of curiosity. Spanish guidelines protect the animals, so the captain will slow down and avoid cutting across a pod or separating mothers from calves.

Is private dolphin hire suitable for young children?

Yes, calmer catamarans and day cruisers suit families well, with shade, railings and life jackets provided. Morning departures usually mean flatter seas, which is gentler for children and anyone prone to seasickness on the open water.

Do I need to bring anything?

Bring sun cream, a hat, water and a light layer for the breeze offshore. Many boats supply snorkelling gear and towels, and your skipper can confirm what is on board when you message before the trip.

Marbella's resident dolphins make a private charter one of the most memorable ways to spend a morning on the Costa del Sol in 2026. Compare boats, capacities and departure marinas, then browse the listings to find the right skippered trip for your group and dates.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
